overview: Jeskai Tempo in Legacy leverages white, blue, and red to control the board while maintaining a steady stream of card advantage. By cycling through Ponder and Flow State, you smooth your draws and adapt to the opponent's strategy without falling behind. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provides a resilient source of value that disrupts opposing game plans while keeping you ahead in resources.
win_conditions: Outdrawing opponents with Ponder and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student
Closing games with efficient threats accelerated by Flow State
Disrupting opponent's key plays with cheap interaction and card selection

keep_criteria: Hands containing Ponder or Flow State for turn one or two
Hands with early disruption and a land drop
Hands featuring Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student alongside interaction
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (prioritizing duals or fixing), Ponder or another card-draw engine, and at least one turn-1 or turn-2 interaction (bounce/removal) or a 2-drop threat.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking card draw or interaction
Hands with multiple lands and no spells
Hands without a clear path to turn one or two plays
Mulligan flood, mana screw, or hands with only high-cost spells without fixing or early answers.

early_game: Play lands and cast Ponder to smooth draws
Use cheap interaction to disrupt opponent's early strategy
Establish board presence with efficient creatures
Ponder if opening with 3+ lands or no early plays to dig for a turn-2 threat or interaction.
Turn 2: Cast a 2-drop threat or Flow State/Tamiyo if mana allows, or hold up interaction to protect your board.
Sequence lands to ensure WU or UW availability, and use Ponder to smooth out mana or find answers to early aggression.
mid_game: Deploy Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student to generate card advantage
Cast Flow State to accelerate threats or draw into answers
Maintain tempo by trading efficiently and keeping the board clear
Preserve mana for interaction until threats are established or opponents commit to plays.
Use Ponder, Flow State, and Tamiyo to cycle into answers or threats, but avoid over-digging early.
Manage your graveyard carefully if opponents run graveyard hate, and don't overextend into known sweepers.
Commit threats when you have interaction to protect them or when the opponent is tapped out.
Hold back if they have sweepers, heavy removal, or combo pieces in play.
Use bounce effects to reset key permanents, protect your own threats, or disrupt opponent's mana development.
late_game: Leverage card advantage to outlast opponents
Close out games with resilient threats
Use draw spells to find finishers and stabilize
Resilient creatures, Flow State/Tamiyo bounce loops, or direct damage.
Win by out-valuing in card advantage, protecting a key threat, or closing out with a board state that opponents cannot clear efficiently.
Go all-in when you have card advantage, the opponent has no sweepers or interaction, or you can protect your threat with bounce/removal.
Commit when you've disrupted their win condition and can pressure them into a losing position.

key_cards.name: Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student
key_cards.notes: Play on curve to draw cards and disrupt opponent's board state while maintaining tempo

key_cards.name: Flow State
key_cards.notes: Use to draw cards, accelerate lands, or boost threats based on the current game state

key_cards.name: Ponder
key_cards.notes: Cast on turn one to smooth draws and find key interaction or threats for subsequent turns

sequencing_tips: Hold Ponder for turn one to maximize draw smoothing before committing resources
Cast Flow State before deploying threats to optimize resource efficiency
Use Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student to answer opponent's key plays while drawing cards to stay ahead
Common mistakes include over-digging with Ponder early and flooding out, or using Flow State/Tamiyo bounce on your own creatures unnecessarily.
Always track opponent's mana and graveyard, as Jeskai Tempo relies on precise timing and interaction efficiency.
The synergy between Tamiyo and Flow State is key for card advantage and board control, so sequence them to maximize value without overcommitting.

matchups.advice: Use Ponder to find answers to their disruption, and leverage Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student to outdraw them over the long game.
Prioritize early interaction and use Flow State to accelerate threats or draw into removal to stabilize.
Disrupt their combo pieces early with Ponder and use Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student to stabilize the board.

Use Ponder to dig for answers, cast threats in response to counterspells, or use bounce to protect your own spells. Don't overcommit to one threat; spread your investment and force them to counter multiple spells.

Prioritize early interaction to slow their acceleration. Use bounce/removal to reset key permanents and avoid inefficient trades. Wait for them to overextend or tap out for big spells before committing your own threats.

Win the interaction war by holding up counterspells/bounce for their threats. Use Ponder to find answers and out-value them in card advantage. Win by out-resolving, protecting your threats, and forcing them to overcommit into your interaction.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers, counterspells, and combo win conditions.
Note when opponents tap out for big spells or leave mana open for interaction.
Track graveyard contents against combo decks, and monitor land drops to predict ramp or Tron lock potential.
